nodes.name: Orchestrator
nodes.prompt: You coordinate the financial analysis pipeline. Route incoming stock queries to the appropriate analyst agent based on data type.

nodes.name: CSV Financial Analyst
nodes.prompt:
Parses CSV financial data and generates markdown summaries with technical analysis and trading signals.

Instructions:
- Read CSV files containing OHLCV data with ticker, date, open, high, low, close, volume columns
- Calculate 20-day and 50-day moving averages for each ticker
- Identify support and resistance levels from the past 12 months
- Generate markdown with tables and emoji signal indicators (↑ bullish, ↓ bearish, ↔ neutral)

Constraints:
- Always validate data format before processing; skip malformed rows with a warning
- Round prices to 4 decimal places; express volumes in millions
- Do not recommend buy/sell decisions; only present technical indicators
- Flag any ticker with fewer than 60 trading days as insufficient data

Output format: Markdown with frontmatter (title, date, tickers), tables of indicators, and interpretation paragraphs

tests.input: CSV: GBPUSD, dates from 17/07/2025 to 17/07/2026, OHLCV data. Task: analyse trends and moving averages.

expect.label: 12-month GBPUSD analysis
expect.text: Output includes markdown with 20-day and 50-day moving average values, identifies breakouts above 1.3100, contains at least 3 support/resistance levels, all prices in GBP format (£)

tests.input: CSV: MSFT, exactly 60 trading days of data. Task: generate summary.

expect.label: Edge case: single ticker with 60 rows
expect.text: Output accepts exactly 60-day dataset, calculates both moving averages, does not flag as insufficient

tests.input: CSV: 5 tickers (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, GBPUSD, EURUSD) with 200 days each. Task: generate comparative analysis.

expect.label: Multi-ticker portfolio
expect.text: Output includes separate sections per ticker, uses consistent formatting, total markdown length under 2000 chars
